title: senml: decode ignores trailing bytes after valid pack
description:
File: rust/lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs
Lines: 434-501 (decode function)

After successfully parsing n_recs records, the decode function returns Ok(n_recs) without verifying that all input bytes were consumed. Trailing garbage bytes are silently ignored.

This affects:
1. Record::parse() which expects exactly one record from a complete buffer
2. Detection of malformed or truncated input
3. Security: extra bytes could be an injection attempt

Example: A buffer containing a valid 1-record pack followed by arbitrary bytes will parse successfully, masking the corruption.

Impact: Silent acceptance of malformed input; potential security issue if trailing data is attacker-controlled.

Fix: Add check at end of decode():
```rust
if pos != data.len() {
    return Err(CborError::InvalidInput);  // trailing bytes
}
```
